context: Most travel trouble is just an unmet basic need - a toilet, a glass of water, the wifi code. You can solve almost all of them with one frame plus a noun, so you never have to build a full sentence to get what you need.
explanation: Two openers do the heavy lifting: "necesito" (I need) and "quiero" (I want). Put either one in front of a thing and you've made a clear request. "Necesito" sounds a little more urgent; "quiero" plus "por favor" is the everyday ask.
